name: enumy
summary: Enumy is an ultra fast portable executable that you drop on target Linux machine during a pentest or CTF in the post exploitation phase. Running enumy will enumerate the box for common security vulnerabilities.

documentation.readme: Enumy is an ultra fast portable executable that you drop on target Linux machine during a pentest or CTF in the post exploitation phase. Running enumy will enumerate the box for common security vulnerabilities.
usage_sections: Installation: You can download the final binary from the release x86 or x64 tab. Statically linked to musl Transfer the final enumy binary to the target machine. - latest release

arguments.name: -a
arguments.description: Print all security AUDIT issues to screen (probably won't help duing a CTF)

arguments.name: -d
arguments.description: Print DEBUG mode (1 low, 2 high) to enable error being printed to screen.

arguments.name: -f
arguments.description: run FULL scans (CPU intensive scan's enabled)

arguments.name: -g
arguments.description: print to screen values GREATER than or equal to high, medium & low

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: IGNORE files in this directory (usefull for network shares)

arguments.name: -w
arguments.description: Only WALK files in this directory (usefull for devlopment)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_penetration_testing_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Penetration Testing category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_penetration_testing_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Penetration Testing system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_teaching_example_in_the_prompt_stanza_so_the_llm_can_reason_about_the_right_run_order
function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for enumy (luke-goddard/enumy). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for enumy. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with huntergregal/mimipenguin (score=12); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with volatilityfoundation/volatility3 (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with t6x/reaver-wps-fork-t6x (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: Enumy is an ultra fast portable executable that you drop on target Linux machine during a pentest or CTF in the post exploitation phase. Running enumy will enumerate the box for common security vulnerabilities.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Enumy is an ultra fast portable executable that you drop on target Linux machine during a pentest or CTF in the post exploitation phase. Running enumy will enumerate the box for common security vulnerabilities.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./enumy`. Further invocations from upstream: `./enumy64 -h`; `$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Enumy is an ultra fast portable executable that you drop on target Linux machine during a pentest or CTF in the post exploitation phase. Running enumy will enumerate the box for common security vulnerabilities.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use enumy because Enumy is an ultra fast portable executable that you drop on target Linux machine during a pentest or CTF in the post exploitation phase. Running enumy will enum.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./enumy` as documented upstream for enumy.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./enumy64 -h` as documented upstream for enumy.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for enumy.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair enumy with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `enumy --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
